title: How to Fix Your Entire Life in 1 Day · 一日重启协议
author: Dan Koe · 译：CSDN/语山君

pairs.en: You're probably going to quit your new years resolution.
pairs.zh: 你或许很快就会放弃自己的新年决心。

pairs.en: And that's okay. Most people do (studies show 80-90% failure rates) because most people don't actually want to change on a deep, internal level.
pairs.zh: 这没什么大不了的。大多数人都是如此（研究显示失败率高达80%-90%），因为大多数人并非发自内心深处渴望改变。

pairs.en: They go about changing their life in the completely wrong way.
pairs.zh: 他们改变人生的方式从一开始就完全错了。

pairs.en: Human nature is a bitch, and the worst feeling is when you make a promise to yourself and can't help but break it.
pairs.zh: 人性本就复杂难缠，而最糟糕的感受，莫过于向自己许下承诺，却身不由己地违背它。

pairs.en: You start to feel helpless, and if you don't know what you're doing, you may continue the cycle for years on end: always wanting to change, but never being able to.
pairs.zh: 你会开始感到无助，若始终不得其法，这种循环可能会持续数年：一直渴望改变，却永远无法迈出实质性的一步。

pairs.en: You aren't where you want to be because you aren't the person who would be there.
pairs.zh: 你到不了想去的地方，因为你还不是能站在那里的人。

pairs.en: Changing your actions to make progress toward the goal (least important, second order).
pairs.zh: 改变行为，朝着目标推进（相对次要，属于次级因素）。

pairs.en: Changing who you are so that your behavior naturally follows (most important, first order).
pairs.zh: 改变自我，让行为自然而然随之转变（至关重要，属于核心因素）。

pairs.en: Most people set a surface-level goal, hype themselves up to remain disciplined for the first few weeks, then go back to their old ways without much struggle, because they were trying to build a great life on a rotting foundation.
pairs.zh: 大多数人只会设定流于表面的目标，在最初几周给自己打鸡血强行自律，而后便毫不费力地回到老样子——因为他们试图在腐朽的根基之上，搭建所谓的美好人生。

pairs.en: If you want a specific outcome in life, you must have the lifestyle that creates that outcome long before you reach it.
pairs.zh: 若你渴望人生达成某个特定目标，那么在实现它之前，你必须先拥有能造就这个结果的生活方式。

pairs.en: When you truly change yourself, all of your habits that don't move the needle toward your goal become disgusting.
pairs.zh: 当你真正实现自我蜕变后，所有无助于目标达成的习惯都会变得令人厌恶。

pairs.en: Trust only movement. Life happens at the level of events, not of words.
pairs.zh: 只相信行动。生活发生在事件的层面，而非言语的层面。

pairs.en: Real change, at its core, is about changing your subconscious goals.
pairs.zh: 真正的改变，其核心在于改变你潜意识里的目标。

pairs.en: You aren't where you want to be because you don't want to be there — your subconscious goals are protecting you from the judgment that comes with finishing and publishing your work.
pairs.zh: 你并非真的想去那里——你的潜意识目标在保护你免受完成和发表作品带来的评判。

pairs.en: When your identity is threatened, your body reacts the same as a physical threat — fight or flight.
pairs.zh: 当你的身份受到威胁时，你的身体反应和面对物理威胁一样——战或逃。

pairs.en: You set a goal → perceive reality through its lens → notice helpful info → act & get feedback → behavior becomes automatic → it merges into identity → you DEFEND that identity → identity shapes new goals.
pairs.zh: 设定目标 → 通过目标滤镜感知现实 → 注意到有帮助的信息 → 行动并接收反馈 → 行为自动化 → 融入自我身份 → 捍卫这个身份 → 身份塑造新目标。

sections.title: Part VI · 一日重启协议

pairs.en: Describe the vague, lingering dissatisfaction you've learned to live with.
pairs.zh: 描述那种你已经学会忍受的、平淡却挥之不去的不满。

pairs.en: What do you complain about repeatedly but never change?
pairs.zh: 你反复抱怨却从不改变的是什么？

pairs.en: What truth about your current life are you terrified to tell someone you deeply respect?
pairs.zh: 关于你当前的生活，有什么真相是你害怕告诉一个你深深敬重的人的？

pairs.en: Describe an ordinary Tuesday 5 years from now if nothing changes.
pairs.zh: 如果5年什么都不变，描述一个普通的周二。

pairs.en: If you could snap your fingers and live a different life in 3 years, what would an ordinary Tuesday look like?
pairs.zh: 如果你能打个响指在3年后过上另一种生活，一个普通的周二会是什么样？

pairs.en: Am I walking toward the life I dread or the life I desire?
pairs.zh: 我在走向我讨厌的人生，还是我渴望的人生？

pairs.en: When today was I most alive? When was I most numb?
pairs.zh: 今天什么时候最有活力？什么时候最麻木？

pairs.en: One-sentence anti-vision: what will I NOT let my life become?
pairs.zh: 一句话反愿景：我绝不让我的人生变成什么。

pairs.en: One-sentence minimum viable vision: what am I building?
pairs.zh: 一句话最小可行愿景：我正在建设什么。

pairs.en: 1-year goal → 1-month project → 2-3 daily leverage actions
pairs.zh: 1年目标 → 1月项目 → 每日2-3个杠杆行动。

pairs.en: Victory Condition → Vision
pairs.zh: 胜利条件 → 愿景

pairs.en: Failure Penalty → Anti-Vision
pairs.zh: 失败惩罚 → 反愿景

pairs.en: Main Quest → 1-Year Goal
pairs.zh: 主线任务 → 1年目标

pairs.en: Boss Fight → 1-Month Project
pairs.zh: Boss战 → 1月项目

pairs.en: Daily Quests → Daily Leverage Actions
pairs.zh: 每日任务 → 每日杠杆行动

pairs.en: Game Rules → Non-Negotiable Constraints
pairs.zh: 游戏规则 → 绝不牺牲的底线

pairs.en: Goals are not endpoints — they are a cognitive lens. Happiness and satisfaction lie in the process of continuous progress.
pairs.zh: 目标并非为了「达成」而设定——它是一种认知透镜。快乐与满足，藏在不断进步的过程中。

pairs.en: The only real test of intelligence is if you get what you want out of life.
pairs.zh: 衡量一个人是否真正聪明的唯一标准，就是看他能不能搞到自己想要的人生。
